Title: Subfunctionalization of a monolignol to a phytoalexin glucosyltransferase is accompanied by substrate inhibition

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Family 1 UGT; GT1 glycosyltransferase; GT-B fold; PSPG box; GSS motif
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Scopoletin; umbelliferone; vanillin; carvacrol; coniferyl alcohol; coniferyl aldehyde; sinapyl alcohol; sinapyl aldehyde; 4-coumaryl alcohol; 4-coumaryl aldehyde
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose from UDP-glucose to phenolic acceptors including scopoletin and monolignols, forming glucosides; monolignols were converted exclusively to 4-O-glucosides
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms glycosylated terminal products and detoxified/storage forms
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established; functionally important control point for scopoletin detoxification due to strong substrate inhibition
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Stem for monolignol-related function; likely pathogen-challenged leaf tissues for scopoletin glucoside context
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Detoxification/storage of scopoletin; modulation of phytoalexin levels; lignin-related monolignol glycosylation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Primarily expressed in stem tissue; constitutive stem expression
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Pathogen attack/hypersensitive response context discussed for scopoletin accumulation; in vitro enzymology with scopoletin and monolignols
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes scopoletin glucosylation with unusually strong substrate inhibition at low micromolar scopoletin; also glucosylates monolignols efficiently
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Recombinant protein assays in E. coli; UDP-Glo kinetics; LC-MS product identification; HDX-MS; homology modeling; docking; site-directed mutagenesis; chimeric mutant analysis
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Expression data referenced from Sun et al. 2019; no new omics accession for this gene in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Strong substrate inhibition reduces activity at higher scopoletin concentrations; multifunctionality may constrain optimization
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for engineering scopoletin glucosylation/detoxification and phenylpropanoid glycosides; residues such as F87 and regions F155-S191 can be edited to modulate substrate inhibition without abolishing monolignol activity
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Open/closed conformers were modeled; a second allosteric scopoletin-binding site is proposed near the catalytic center; F87 and R91 are key for substrate inhibition; H390 affects catalysis and inhibition; likely subfunctionalized from promiscuous monolignol UGT
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NbUGT72AY1 is a promiscuous UGT that efficiently glucosylates monolignols and especially scopoletin. 2. It shows unusually strong partial uncompetitive substrate inhibition with scopoletin. 3. HDX-MS, docking, morphing, and mutagenesis support a two-site model in which a second scopoletin molecule binds near the catalytic site and blocks product release. 4. F87, R91, H390, and the F155-S191 region contribute to promiscuity and substrate inhibition. 5. The enzyme likely contributes to both lignin-related monolignol metabolism and scopoletin glucoside formation in Nicotiana.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Family 1 UGT; GT1 glycosyltransferase; GT-B fold; PSPG box; GSS motif
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Phenylpropanoid/monolignol glycosylation; weak side activity toward scopoletin glucosylation
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Sinapyl aldehyde; sinapyl alcohol; coniferyl alcohol; coniferyl aldehyde; vanillin; scopoletin; carvacrol
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose from UDP-glucose to monolignols and related phenolics, producing 4-O-glucosides; shows weaker substrate inhibition with scopoletin than NbUGT72AY1
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms glycosylated phenylpropanoid products and side-product scopoletin glucoside
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Likely lignin precursor homeostasis; weak hydroxycoumarin glucosylation side activity
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Functions mainly as monolignol glucosyltransferase with weak scopoletin substrate inhibition; reverse mutations can increase scopoletin glucosylation and induce stronger inhibition
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Recombinant protein assays; UDP-Glo kinetics; LC-MS; comparative mutagenesis and chimeric swaps relative to NbUGT72AY1
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Nine peroxidases mentioned in discussion for potato enzyme interaction, but not experimentally analyzed in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Expression source from EBI Gene Expression Atlas experiment E-MTAB-552
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Engineered increased scopoletin activity is accompanied by substrate inhibition; chimeric mutant can lose activity
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential scaffold for engineering monolignol vs scopoletin glucosylation balance; specific substitutions such as V83I_I84F or Y389H can enhance scopoletin glucosylation while retaining monolignol activity
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used as close homolog comparator to infer subfunctionalization of NbUGT72AY1; shows possible UDP-glucose hydrolase side activity in UDP-Glo assay
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. StUGT72AY2 is a close potato homolog with high sequence identity to NbUGT72AY1 but different substrate preference and weaker substrate inhibition. 2. It preferentially glucosylates monolignol-related phenolics and behaves more like a canonical monolignol UGT. 3. Reverse mutations increasing scopoletin activity also introduce substrate inhibition, supporting evolutionary subfunctionalization toward a scopoletin-active enzyme.
